friction_items.description: audit/audit_and_validation runs `audit_assumptions` (Cox PH, residuals, missing-data), `audit_figures` (DPI + .caption.md + .summary.md sidecars + SVG companion + aspect-ratio sanity), `audit_power`, `audit_evalue` — none of which apply to an 8-participant interview study. The protocol's step_intent: analyse + figure_required: false in qualitative_research's create_step auto-waives the figure gate, but the audit protocol still walks the auditor through ~6 sections of inapplicable doctrine. provenance_completeness is the most acute mismatch: it demands `.prov.json` sidecars with rng_seed / sha256 / software.packages for every workspace output, but the qualitative deliverables are codebook.md, themes.md, reflexivity.md, member_check responses, redaction ledgers — prose + tables, not numerics. An auditor opens provenance_audit.md and finds either 0% coverage (BLOCKER barring synthesis) or has to hand-carve qualitative outputs out of the inventory before the master audit will run.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Make audit_and_validation domain-aware: read researcher_config.domain + step_summary.step_intent and skip / replace inapplicable steps with qualitative equivalents (saturation_audit, intercoder_audit, member_checking_audit, quote_anonymisation_audit, redaction_completeness_audit). Define a qualitative sidecar schema for provenance_completeness: codebook_version / coder_count / transcript_count / pseudonym_map_sha256 / redaction_ledger_sha256 / consent_form_version. Have provenance_completeness consume that schema when step_intent is qualitative.

friction_items.description: Qualitative rigor is checked in THREE overlapping places: (1) methodology/qualitative_quality_audit (saturation curve, reflexivity, intercoder κ, member checking, quote anon, audit trail) — a PROTOCOL, no enforcing tool; (2) qualitative/validity/member_checking — pack protocol enforcing only member-checking with `divergences.md`, `contact_log.md`; (3) qualitative_report_format coverage_gate that does COREQ/SRQR walking + percentage floor. None of the three calls tool_audit_claims, tool_grounding_verify, or tool_audit_quality_full. The master audit (tool_audit_quality_full) does not know about saturation, kappa, member checking, or quote anonymisation — they are protocol-prose gates with no machine-enforceable hook. From the auditor's seat the rigor verdict is split across three .md files in three locations and never rolled up into the master audit_report.md.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Ship tool_qualitative_quality_audit as a first-class auditor surface that reads codebook.md + themes.md + reflexivity.md + member_check responses + redaction ledger + COREQ/SRQR coverage and emits a single structured blockers array. Wire it into audit/audit_and_validation as `audit_qualitative` step (parallel to `audit_assumptions`). Make tool_audit_quality_full chain it when the project domain is qualitative.

friction_items.description: 226 tools total; 42 are audit-relevant (tool_audit_synthesis, tool_audit_power, tool_audit_assumptions, tool_audit_figure, tool_audit_figure_full, tool_audit_figure_interactivity, tool_audit_figure_coverage, tool_audit_citations, tool_audit_reproducibility, tool_audit_step_completeness, tool_audit_step_literature, tool_audit_findings_query, tool_audit_findings_diff, tool_audit_version_coherence, tool_audit_code_quality, tool_audit_prose, tool_audit_claims, tool_audit_evalue, tool_audit_quality_full, tool_audit_coherence, tool_audit_cliches, tool_audit_cross_deliverable_consistency, tool_audit_reviewer_responses, tool_audit_dashboard_content, tool_grounding_register, tool_grounding_verify, tool_ground_from_context, tool_claim_verify, tool_discussion_coverage_audit, tool_preregister_freeze, tool_preregister_diff, tool_redteam_review, tool_response_to_reviewers, tool_citations_verify, tool_reliability_report, tool_reviewer_simulate, tool_rebuttal_draft, tool_qualitative_quote_provenance, tool_dashboard_reviewer_sim, tool_null_findings_report, tool_reviewer_response_compile, tool_verify). For an 8-participant interview study the auditor needs maybe 5 (tool_audit_quality_full, tool_audit_citations, tool_grounding_verify, tool_audit_coherence, a missing tool_qualitative_quality_audit). The other 37 are noise that bloats AI context. Worse, 89 tools have NO `short` field set — so list_tools falls back to the first sentence of `description`, which for some tools means raw JSON-schema parameter blurb leaks into the auditor's startup payload.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Phase 9 consolidation: collapse the figure trio (tool_audit_figure / tool_audit_figure_full / tool_audit_figure_coverage / tool_audit_figure_interactivity) into a single tool with mode=basic|full|coverage|interactivity; collapse grounding (tool_grounding_register + tool_ground_from_context + tool_grounding_verify) into tool_ground with op=register|verify|from_context; collapse claim-checking (tool_claim_verify + tool_audit_claims + tool_grounding_verify) — document the relationship at minimum. Promote tool_audit_quality_full as the SINGLE auditor entry point and demote per-dimension tools to advanced surface. Backfill `short` for the 89 missing tools so list_tools doesn't leak raw description sentences.

friction_items.description: tool_audit_step_completeness returns status='error' when any step has BLOCKERS, and tool_synthesize refuses to assemble. The description says it writes workspace/logs/step_completeness.md but the JSON envelope returned is `{status: error, error: 'BLOCKED: ...'}`. The auditor has to sys_file_read the log to learn WHICH gate fired WHY. tool_audit_synthesis has the same shape ('default-denies when zero PDFs are present' is documented but the override flag name is not in the error). tool_audit_quality_full aggregates six gates and returns a single error string — the auditor cannot tell from the envelope whether the failure was completeness OR code OR prose OR claims OR prereg OR grounding without a second read. From the auditor's seat the gate output is a black box without follow-up reads.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Every default-deny / blocker tool should return a structured `blockers: [{code, message, fix_hint, override_param, related_artefact_path}]` array in the JSON envelope alongside the human-readable error. The AI surfaces those inline. Pin override flag names + rationale templates in the structured block. For tool_audit_quality_full specifically, return per-dimension verdicts in the envelope so the auditor doesn't need to re-call each sub-tool.

friction_items.description: tool_audit_step_literature is built for STEM steps: workspace/<step>/literature/findings_vs_literature.md with `## Claim:` blocks + Verdict lines AGREES|DISAGREES|EXTENDS|DEFERRED + matching Discussion paragraphs. Qualitative 'literature' is the methodological literature (Braun & Clarke, Lincoln & Guba, Birt et al.) and the substantive secondary literature on the topic — neither maps to per-step AGREES/DISAGREES verdicts on numeric findings. qualitative_research sets literature_required: true in step_summary.yaml so the gate fires, but the file format does not match what the gate parses. tool_discussion_coverage_audit then BLOCKs because non-AGREES verdicts have no corresponding Discussion paragraphs. The auditor either sees false BLOCKERS or has to override the gate per step.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add a qualitative branch to tool_audit_step_literature: when step_intent is qualitative + secondary_lit_path is set, parse a flatter qualitative ledger format (citation + how_it_informed_codebook + how_it_appears_in_discussion) instead of verdict-keyed prose. OR introduce tool_qualitative_secondary_lit_audit as the qualitative-side gate, wired into audit_and_validation, and waive tool_audit_step_literature when step_intent matches.

friction_items.description: From the auditor's seat the single most-impactful artefacts in this scenario are: inputs/private/pseudonym_map.csv (gitignored — auditor cannot read it; rightly so), workspace/<step>/outputs/reports/redaction_ledger_*.csv (the only reproducibility trail for de-identification), workspace/member_checks/round_<N>/divergences.md (the only trail of dialogic revisions). None of these are covered by the provenance_completeness schema (no rng_seed; no sha256; no `produced_by.script`). The protocol explicitly exempts the pseudonym map from provenance because it's gitignored — but a year-from-now auditor has no way to verify that today's redacted transcripts are byte-identical to a re-run from the same raw transcripts + same map. The deterministic-re-run claim (`redaction_ledger_is_deterministic_re_runnable: true` in qualitative_pii_redaction.quality_bar) has no machine verification.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add a qualitative-provenance sidecar schema (pseudonym_map_sha256 / policy_sha256 / presidio_version / spacy_model_version / coder_id / coding_session_id) and have a tool_qualitative_provenance_verify check that re-running detect_and_redact against the same raw transcripts + map produces a byte-identical ledger sha256. Wire it into provenance_completeness via a `qualitative_provenance:` block.

friction_items.description: pre_submission_checklist.yaml step `override_audit_review` reads workspace/logs/override_log.md and flags UNRESOLVED bypasses with rationale not mirrored in cover_letter.md. For a qualitative submission, the cover_letter format differs from STEM; the auditor needs to know whether the cross-check applies. More acute: the qualitative pipeline structurally bypasses several STEM gates via step_intent: analyse + figure_required: false in step_summary.yaml. There is no concept of 'gate auto-waived by step_intent: analyse on a qualitative step' in the override log — so the auto-waivers are invisible to pre_submission_checklist and the auditor cannot distinguish AUTO-WAIVED (by intent) from MANUAL-OVERRIDE (researcher passed override_completeness_gate=true) from UNRESOLVED (genuine bypass).
friction_items.suggested_fix: Distinguish AUTO-WAIVED from MANUAL-OVERRIDE from UNRESOLVED. Auto-waivers go in step_summary.yaml under `gates_waived: [figure_gate: 'analyse intent + figure_required=false', power_gate: 'qualitative method', evalue_gate: 'non-causal']`. pre_submission_checklist reads both surfaces (step_summary + override_log) and rolls them up with different severity bands. Surface the gate provenance in the master audit_report.md so the auditor sees 'figure_gate WAIVED by step_intent' instead of 'figure_gate not run'.

friction_items.description: Four audit / validation protocols live in audit/: audit_and_validation, pre_submission_checklist, provenance_completeness — plus the master tool tool_audit_quality_full + discussion_coverage_audit. From the auditor's seat the question is which to run when. The next_protocol chains are correct (provenance → audit_and_validation → synthesis → pre_submission_checklist) but undocumented as a single visual flow. audit_and_validation step 1 (audit_master) is marked PREFERRED but the protocol still walks the AI through 7 component steps; the auditor reads them anyway and burns turns deciding whether to call them individually.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add audit_flow.md to docs/ showing the audit DAG with which artefacts each consumes/produces. Or auto-generate from next_protocol chains via a `sys_audit_dag` tool. In audit_and_validation, mark the per-component steps as `mode: debug_only` so the AI sees them only on explicit request — the master step is the default path.

friction_items.description: qualitative_quality_audit step intercoder_agreement_check expects Cohen's κ / Krippendorff's α from ≥2 coders. tool_qualitative_codebook_diff also computes per-code κ from two rounds of applied codings. For the typical 8-participant interview study run by ONE researcher (the common case for psychology / nursing / education dissertation work), there is no second coder — κ is undefined, and Braun & Clarke's reflexive-TA tradition explicitly REJECTS reporting κ in a reflexive frame (thematic_analysis_braun_clarke.editorial_voice rule 2). The auditor either sees a false BLOCKER ('κ not reported') OR has to read three protocols to figure out that reflexive TA waives the gate. The audit chain does not encode the epistemological branch.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add a `coding_paradigm` field to study_design.yaml: codebook_TA (κ required) | reflexive_TA (κ rejected by doctrine) | grounded_theory (theoretical sampling, no κ) | framework (per-domain). Have tool_qualitative_quality_audit branch on the paradigm: codebook → require κ ≥ 0.7; reflexive → require trustworthiness criteria (Lincoln & Guba); grounded → require theoretical saturation memo. Encode the branch in YAML, not in protocol prose.

friction_items.description: redaction_ledger_*.csv is produced per transcript by qualitative_pii_redaction.detect_and_redact (schema: line,start,end,original_hash,class,replacement,score,requires_review). The audit step samples ≥5% of lines and computes false_negatives / false_positives / NER_recall, writing redaction_audit.md. From the auditor's seat the .md is human-readable but the audit chain does not consume it: provenance_completeness does not look for redaction_audit.md; audit_and_validation does not roll up false_negatives; pre_submission_checklist does not flag a non-zero false_negatives count. A consent-breach bug in the redaction pipeline (a name slipping through) propagates to themes.md → paper.md → submission with no machine-checkable signal.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Ship tool_audit_redaction (reads workspace/<step>/outputs/reports/redaction_audit.md + redaction_ledger_*.csv, returns blockers: [{false_negative_count, ner_recall_below_threshold, requires_review_unresolved}]). Wire it into audit_and_validation as `audit_redaction` step and into pre_submission_checklist as a GREEN/YELLOW/RED contributor. Any false_negative > 0 = RED.

friction_items.description: From the auditor's view, four grounding-flavored tools (tool_grounding_register, tool_grounding_verify, tool_ground_from_context, tool_claim_verify) overlap on semantics. tool_grounding_verify is the gate ('every decision in analysis.md must carry a grounding record'). tool_grounding_register writes a grounding record. tool_ground_from_context is conditional dispatch. tool_claim_verify is Chain-of-Verification (CoVe) Q&A. The relationship is documented nowhere and the noun overload (ground / grounding / claim) hurts. For a qualitative study the auditor wants to verify that every theme in themes.md is grounded in quote(s) from a participant — which is the qualitative-side analogue of tool_grounding_verify, but no protocol wires that up.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Document the verification surface in docs/AUDIT_SURFACE.md: one paragraph per tool with when-to-use, in-out shapes, who-calls-whom. Consolidate tool_grounding_register + tool_grounding_verify + tool_ground_from_context into tool_ground with op=register|verify|from_context (the dispatcher already routes on `not arguments.get('claim')` — make it explicit). Promote tool_audit_claims as the umbrella that chains all three for the synthesis-cited-numbers case.

friction_items.description: thematic_analysis_braun_clarke.yaml + member_checking.yaml + qualitative_pii_redaction.yaml are dense — each protocol's description block is 15-35 lines of doctrine (Braun & Clarke 2019 reflexive update, Lincoln & Guba trustworthiness criteria, HIPAA Safe Harbor 18-class enumeration, GDPR Article 4(5)). Beautiful for a domain researcher; intimidating for an auditor whose job is to verify rigor, not to interpret reflexive TA history. The auditor wants a one-line `what this protocol promises + what artefacts it produces` header.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add a `summary:` field (already used in _router_index.yaml entries) to each protocol's top matter that distills the description to ~3 sentences for the auditor. Render `summary` first in sys_protocol_get; collapse the doctrine into a `rationale:` block the auditor can skip.

friction_items.description: The qualitative pack ships only 3 tools (codebook_diff, quote_provenance, select_standard). All three are scaffold writers — they write a .md or .csv but do not return a structured pass/fail to the master audit. The 5 qualitative pack protocols (thematic_analysis_braun_clarke, grounded_theory_iteration, coding_scheme_iteration, member_checking, qualitative_report_format) outnumber the tools 5:3, and the audit gates lean entirely on generic STEM-flavored tools (tool_audit_step_completeness, tool_audit_synthesis). The auditor has no qualitative-side machine-checkable surface.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Ship 3-4 qualitative-side audit tools in v2: tool_qualitative_quality_audit (umbrella), tool_qualitative_redaction_audit, tool_qualitative_saturation_audit, tool_qualitative_member_checking_audit. Wire each into audit_and_validation. Drops the auditor's reliance on per-protocol prose for rigor verdicts.

convoluted_tools: tool_audit_figure / tool_audit_figure_full / tool_audit_figure_coverage / tool_audit_figure_interactivity — four near-identical figure auditors; an auditor cannot tell at a glance which is the full one

tool_grounding_register / tool_grounding_verify / tool_ground_from_context — three grounding-flavored tools with a hidden dispatcher branch on `not arguments.get('claim')`; the relationship is not stated in any tool description

tool_audit_quality_full vs tool_audit_step_completeness vs tool_audit_step_literature — overlapping scopes; tool_audit_quality_full ALREADY chains the others but they are still listed as standalone in audit_and_validation steps, so the auditor sees them twice

tool_claim_verify (CoVe Q&A) vs tool_audit_claims (every number traces to a workspace output) vs tool_grounding_verify (every decision carries a grounding record) — three claim-checking surfaces with different semantics; the relationship is documented nowhere

tool_qualitative_quote_provenance (one tool call per quote) vs tool_audit_claims (extracts numbers from paper.md) — the qualitative analogue (every quote in themes.md is in the registry) does not exist as a tool; the auditor has to read qualitative_report_format prose to know it's enforced

tool_redteam_review vs tool_reviewer_simulate vs tool_dashboard_reviewer_sim vs tool_reviewer_response_compile vs tool_response_to_reviewers vs tool_rebuttal_draft — six reviewer-flavored tools with overlapping noun-orderings; the auditor cannot infer call order from the names
confusing_protocols: audit/audit_and_validation — step 1 (audit_master) is marked PREFERRED but the protocol still walks the AI through 7 more component steps; the auditor reads them as a mandatory checklist instead of a debug-only fallback

audit/provenance_completeness — STEM-centric sidecar schema (rng_seed / sha256 / software.packages) applied uniformly to qualitative prose + ledger outputs; the protocol does not call out qualitative exemptions or a qualitative sidecar schema
audit/pre_submission_checklist — long (353 lines, 10 steps); reads more like a doctrine essay than a checklist; the auditor cannot quickly scan for what's actually being gated

methodology/qualitative_quality_audit — promises saturation / reflexivity / intercoder / member-checking / quote-anon / audit-trail gates but ALL are protocol-prose with no enforcing tool; the auditor cannot verify any of them via tool_audit_quality_full

methodology/qualitative_pii_redaction — promises a tool_qualitative_pii_redact MCP wrapper but the protocol's detect_and_redact step still tells the AI to write its own scripts/qual_pii_redact.py via tool_python_exec ('a dedicated first-class MCP wrapper is planned but not yet shipped; see docs/ROADMAP.md'); the auditor cannot tell whether the de-identification trail is machine-checkable

qualitative/output/qualitative_report_format coverage_gate vs methodology/qualitative_quality_audit — both walk COREQ/SRQR; the relationship is unclear (one is in the core, one in the pack; which fires when?)
recommendations: Ship tool_qualitative_quality_audit as a first-class auditor surface that reads codebook.md + themes.md + reflexivity.md + member-checking artefacts + redaction ledger + COREQ/SRQR coverage and emits a structured blockers array. Wire it into audit/audit_and_validation as `audit_qualitative` step. Promote tool_audit_quality_full to chain it when domain is qualitative.

Define a qualitative provenance sidecar schema (pseudonym_map_sha256 / policy_sha256 / presidio_version / spacy_model_version / coder_id / coding_session_id) and update provenance_completeness to consume it. Ship tool_qualitative_provenance_verify that re-runs detect_and_redact against the same raw + map and confirms byte-identical ledger sha256.

Backfill `short` for the 89 tools missing it. The list_tools fallback (first sentence of description, capped 160 chars) is leaking raw JSON-schema parameter blurb into the auditor's startup payload for tools without short — a 226-tool startup with even 10% noise is painful.

Phase 9 consolidation: collapse the figure auditor trio + the grounding trio into single tools with op= / mode= params. Document the 6-tool reviewer-flavored surface in docs/AUDIT_SURFACE.md with when-to-use, in-out shapes, call order.

Make every default-deny / blocker tool return a structured `blockers: [{code, message, fix_hint, override_param, related_artefact_path}]` array in the JSON envelope alongside the human-readable error. The auditor surfaces blockers inline without a follow-up sys_file_read.

Distinguish AUTO-WAIVED (by step_intent / figure_required=false / qualitative method) from MANUAL-OVERRIDE (researcher passed override flag) from UNRESOLVED (genuine bypass with no rationale) in the override log. Surface waiver provenance in audit_report.md so the auditor sees 'figure_gate WAIVED by step_intent' instead of 'figure_gate not run'.

Add a `coding_paradigm:` field to study_design.yaml (codebook_TA | reflexive_TA | grounded_theory | framework) and have tool_qualitative_quality_audit branch on the paradigm: codebook → require κ ≥ 0.7; reflexive → require trustworthiness criteria; grounded → require theoretical saturation. Encode the epistemological branch in YAML, not in protocol prose.

Ship tool_audit_redaction that consumes workspace/<step>/outputs/reports/redaction_audit.md + redaction_ledger_*.csv and returns structured blockers. Wire it into audit_and_validation. Any false_negative > 0 = RED in pre_submission_checklist.

Add a `summary:` field to every protocol's top matter (already in _router_index.yaml) — ~3 sentences for the auditor. Render `summary` first in sys_protocol_get; collapse doctrine into a `rationale:` block the auditor can skip.

Mark per-component steps in audit_and_validation as `mode: debug_only` so the AI's default path is the master step (audit_master). The 7 component steps are currently walked alongside the master, doubling the auditor's reading load.

Add audit_flow.md to docs/ showing the audit DAG (provenance_completeness → audit_and_validation → discussion_coverage → synthesis → pre_submission_checklist) with which artefacts each consumes/produces. Or auto-generate via a sys_audit_dag tool.

Ship the promised tool_qualitative_pii_redact MCP wrapper (presidio + spaCy + regex + deterministic pseudonym map) so qualitative_pii_redaction.detect_and_redact stops punting to a hand-authored scripts/qual_pii_redact.py. The current state means the de-identification trail is researcher-authored and the auditor cannot verify the pipeline ran the same way twice.
